title: Inorder Successor/Predecessor in BST
content:
Problem Statement: Given a Binary Search Tree and a ‘key’ value which represents the data data of a node in this tree. Return the inorder predecessor and successor of the given node in the BST.

  • The predecessor of a node in BST is that node that will be visited just before the given node in the inorder traversal of the tree. Return nullptr if the given node is the one that is visited first in the inorder traversal.
  • The successor of a node in BST is that node that will be visited immediately after the given node in the inorder traversal of the tree. Return nullptr if the given node is visited last in the inorder traversal.

    • Examples 
    Example 1:
Input: Binary Search Tree: 5 3 7 2 4 6 9 1 -1 -1 -1 -1 -1 8 10, Key = 8
Output: Inorder Predecessor: 7, Inorder Successor: 9
Explanation: 

    
Example 2:
Input: Binary Search Tree: 5 2 7 1 4 6 9 -1 -1 3 -1 -1 -1 8 10, Key = 3
Output: Inorder Predecessor: 2, Inorder Successor: 4
Explanation:

    Disclaimer: It's highly recommend trying to solve it before looking at the solution.

    Brute Force
    Algorithm
    • First, we traverse the binary search tree (BST) in an in-order fashion to obtain the sorted sequence of node values. This way, we can directly use the sequence to determine the next node greater than the given key.
    • Once we have the in-order traversal of the BST, we can perform a binary search on this sorted list. The binary search will allow us to efficiently find the first value that is greater than the given key. This value is the in-order successor.
    • If the binary search finds a matching value, we return it as the successor. If not, we continue searching to identify the next value in the sorted sequence that is greater than the key.

    Code
    123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536373839404142434445464748495051525354555657585960616263646566676869707172737475767778798081828384858687888990919293949596979899100101102103104105106107108109110111
    #include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

// define tree node structure
struct TreeNode {
    // value of the node
    int val;
    // pointer to the left child
    TreeNode* left;
    // pointer to the right child
    TreeNode* right;
    // constructor
    TreeNode(int x) : val(x), left(nullptr), right(nullptr) {}
};

// solution class
class Solution {
public:
    // find inorder successor by building inorder list and binary searching
    TreeNode* inorderSuccessor(TreeNode* root, TreeNode* p) {
        // container for inorder traversal
        vector<int> inorder;
        // fill inorder list
        inorderTraversal(root, inorder);
        // locate index of p->val (or insertion index)
        int idx = binarySearch(inorder, p->val);
        // handle edge cases: last element or invalid
        if (idx == (int)inorder.size() - 1 || idx == -1) {
            return nullptr;
        }
        // return a new node with successor value (mirroring given logic)
        return new TreeNode(inorder[idx + 1]);
    }

    // inorder traversal helper
    void inorderTraversal(TreeNode* root, vector<int>& inorder) {
        // base case
        if (root == nullptr) return;
        // traverse left
        inorderTraversal(root->left, inorder);
        // visit node
        inorder.push_back(root->val);
        // traverse right
        inorderTraversal(root->right, inorder);
    }

    // binary search helper
    int binarySearch(vector<int>& arr, int target) {
        // search bounds
        int left = 0, right = (int)arr.size() - 1;
        // standard binary search
        while (left <= right) {
            // midpoint
            int mid = left + (right - left) / 2;
            // found case
            if (arr[mid] == target) return mid;
            // move right
            else if (arr[mid] < target) left = mid + 1;
            // move left
            else right = mid - 1;
        }
        // not found: return insertion index or -1 if at end
        return left == (int)arr.size() ? -1 : left;
    }
};

// inorder print helper
void printInOrder(TreeNode* root) {
    // base case
    if (root == nullptr) return;
    // left
    printInOrder(root->left);
    // node
    cout << root->val << " ";
    // right
    printInOrder(root->right);
}

// program entry
int main() {
    // construct BST
    TreeNode* root = new TreeNode(5);
    root->left = new TreeNode(3);
    root->right = new TreeNode(6);
    root->left->left = new TreeNode(2);
    root->left->right = new TreeNode(4);
    root->right->right = new TreeNode(7);

    // show BST inorder
    cout << "BST: ";
    printInOrder(root);
    cout << endl;

    // pick node p (value 4)
    TreeNode* p = root->left->right;

    // compute successor
    Solution solution;
    TreeNode* successor = solution.inorderSuccessor(root, p);

    // print result
    if (successor != nullptr) {
        cout << "Inorder Successor of " << p->val << " is: " << successor->val << endl;
    } else {
        cout << "Inorder Successor of " << p->val << " does not exist." << endl;
    }

    // done
    return 0;
}

    import java.util.*;

// tree node definition
class TreeNode {
    // value of the node
    int val;
    // left child reference
    TreeNode left;
    // right child reference
    TreeNode right;
    // constructor
    TreeNode(int x) { val = x; }
}

// solution class
class Solution {
    // find inorder successor by building inorder list and binary searching
    public TreeNode inorderSuccessor(TreeNode root, TreeNode p) {
        // container for inorder traversal
        List<Integer> inorder = new ArrayList<>();
        // fill inorder list
        inorderTraversal(root, inorder);
        // locate index of p.val (or insertion index)
        int idx = binarySearch(inorder, p.val);
        // handle edge cases: last element or invalid
        if (idx == inorder.size() - 1 || idx == -1) {
            return null;
        }
        // return a new node with successor value (mirroring given logic)
        return new TreeNode(inorder.get(idx + 1));
    }

    // inorder traversal helper
    private void inorderTraversal(TreeNode root, List<Integer> inorder) {
        // base case
        if (root == null) return;
        // traverse left
        inorderTraversal(root.left, inorder);
        // visit node
        inorder.add(root.val);
        // traverse right
        inorderTraversal(root.right, inorder);
    }

    // binary search helper
    private int binarySearch(List<Integer> arr, int target) {
        // search bounds
        int left = 0, right = arr.size() - 1;
        // standard binary search
        while (left <= right) {
            // midpoint
            int mid = left + (right - left) / 2;
            // found case
            if (arr.get(mid) == target) return mid;
            // move right
            else if (arr.get(mid) < target) left = mid + 1;
            // move left
            else right = mid - 1;
        }
        // not found: return insertion index or -1 if at end
        return left == arr.size() ? -1 : left;
    }
}

// separate main class
public class Main {
    // inorder print helper
    static void printInOrder(TreeNode root) {
        // base case
        if (root == null) return;
        // left
        printInOrder(root.left);
        // node
        System.out.print(root.val + " ");
        // right
        printInOrder(root.right);
    }

    // program entry
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        // construct BST
        TreeNode root = new TreeNode(5);
        root.left = new TreeNode(3);
        root.right = new TreeNode(6);
        root.left.left = new TreeNode(2);
        root.left.right = new TreeNode(4);
        root.right.right = new TreeNode(7);

        // show BST inorder
        System.out.print("BST: ");
        printInOrder(root);
        System.out.println();

        // pick node p (value 4)
        TreeNode p = root.left.right;

        // compute successor
        Solution solution = new Solution();
        TreeNode successor = solution.inorderSuccessor(root, p);

        // print result
        if (successor != null) {
            System.out.println("Inorder Successor of " + p.val + " is: " + successor.val);
        } else {
            System.out.println("Inorder Successor of " + p.val + " does not exist.");
        }
    }
}

    # define tree node
class TreeNode:
    # constructor
    def __init__(self, x: int):
        # value of the node
        self.val = x
        # left child reference
        self.left = None
        # right child reference
        self.right = None

# solution class
class Solution:
    # find inorder successor by building inorder list and binary searching
    def inorderSuccessor(self, root: TreeNode, p: TreeNode) -> TreeNode | None:
        # container for inorder traversal
        inorder: list[int] = []
        # fill inorder list
        self.inorderTraversal(root, inorder)
        # locate index of p.val (or insertion index)
        idx = self.binarySearch(inorder, p.val)
        # handle edge cases: last element or invalid
        if idx == len(inorder) - 1 or idx == -1:
            return None
        # return a new node with successor value (mirroring given logic)
        return TreeNode(inorder[idx + 1])

    # inorder traversal helper
    def inorderTraversal(self, root: TreeNode | None, inorder: list[int]) -> None:
        # base case
        if root is None:
            return
        # traverse left
        self.inorderTraversal(root.left, inorder)
        # visit node
        inorder.append(root.val)
        # traverse right
        self.inorderTraversal(root.right, inorder)

    # binary search helper
    def binarySearch(self, arr: list[int], target: int) -> int:
        # search bounds
        left, right = 0, len(arr) - 1
        # standard binary search
        while left <= right:
            # midpoint
            mid = left + (right - left) // 2
            # found case
            if arr[mid] == target:
                return mid
            # move right
            elif arr[mid] < target:
                left = mid + 1
            # move left
            else:
                right = mid - 1
        # not found: return insertion index or -1 if at end
        return -1 if left == len(arr) else left

# inorder print helper
def print_in_order(root: TreeNode | None) -> None:
    # base case
    if root is None:
        return
    # left
    print_in_order(root.left)
    # node
    print(root.val, end=" ")
    # right
    print_in_order(root.right)

# program entry
def main():
    # construct BST
    root = TreeNode(5)
    root.left = TreeNode(3)
    root.right = TreeNode(6)
    root.left.left = TreeNode(2)
    root.left.right = TreeNode(4)
    root.right.right = TreeNode(7)

    # show BST inorder
    print("BST: ", end="")
    print_in_order(root)
    print()

    # pick node p (value 4)
    p = root.left.right

    # compute successor
    solution = Solution()
    successor = solution.inorderSuccessor(root, p)

    # print result
    if successor is not None:
        print(f"Inorder Successor of {p.val} is: {successor.val}")
    else:
        print(f"Inorder Successor of {p.val} does not exist.")

# run main
if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

    // define tree node
class TreeNode {
  // constructor
  constructor(x) {
    // value of the node
    this.val = x;
    // left child reference
    this.left = null;
    // right child reference
    this.right = null;
  }
}

// solution class
class Solution {
  // find inorder successor by building inorder list and binary searching
  inorderSuccessor(root, p) {
    // container for inorder traversal
    const inorder = [];
    // fill inorder list
    this.inorderTraversal(root, inorder);
    // locate index of p.val (or insertion index)
    const idx = this.binarySearch(inorder, p.val);
    // handle edge cases: last element or invalid
    if (idx === inorder.length - 1 || idx === -1) {
      return null;
    }
    // return a new node with successor value (mirroring given logic)
    return new TreeNode(inorder[idx + 1]);
  }

  // inorder traversal helper
  inorderTraversal(root, inorder) {
    // base case
    if (root === null) return;
    // traverse left
    this.inorderTraversal(root.left, inorder);
    // visit node
    inorder.push(root.val);
    // traverse right
    this.inorderTraversal(root.right, inorder);
  }

    // binary search helper
    binarySearch(arr, target) {
        // search bounds
        let left = 0, right = arr.length - 1;
        // standard binary search
        while (left <= right) {
        // midpoint
        const mid = left + Math.floor((right - left) / 2);
        // found case
        if (arr[mid] === target) return mid;
        // move right
        else if (arr[mid] < target) left = mid + 1;
        // move left
        else right = mid - 1;
        }
        // not found: return insertion index or -1 if at end
        return left === arr.length ? -1 : left;
    }
}

// inorder print helper
function printInOrder(root) {
    // base case
    if (root === null) return;
    // left
    printInOrder(root.left);
    // node
    process.stdout.write(root.val + " ");
    // right
    printInOrder(root.right);
}

// program entry
function main() {
    // construct BST
    const root = new TreeNode(5);
    root.left = new TreeNode(3);
    root.right = new TreeNode(6);
    root.left.left = new TreeNode(2);
    root.left.right = new TreeNode(4);
    root.right.right = new TreeNode(7);

    // show BST inorder
    process.stdout.write("BST: ");
    printInOrder(root);
    console.log();

    // pick node p (value 4)
    const p = root.left.right;

    // compute successor
    const solution = new Solution();
    const successor = solution.inorderSuccessor(root, p);

    // print result
    if (successor !== null) {
        console.log("Inorder Successor of " + p.val + " is: " + successor.val);
    } else {
        console.log("Inorder Successor of " + p.val + " does not exist.");
    }
}

// run main
main();
    Complexity Analysis

    Time Complexity: O(N + logN) where N is the number of nodes of the binary search tree. O(N) to traverse all nodes and store them in an inorder array and O(log N) for the binary search.

    Space Complexity: O(N) as an array of size N is used to store the inorder traversal of the binary search tree.

    Better Approach
    Algorithm
    • First, we perform an inorder or Morris traversal of the binary search tree (BST) to visit the nodes in sorted order.
    • While traversing, we check each node and identify the first node with a value greater than the given key.
    • Once we find the node with the greater value, we know it is the inorder successor of the given key, so we return this node.

    Code
    123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536373839404142434445464748495051525354555657585960616263646566676869707172737475767778798081828384
    #include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

// define tree node structure
struct TreeNode {
    // value of the node
    int val;
    // pointer to the left child
    TreeNode* left;
    // pointer to the right child
    TreeNode* right;
    // constructor
    TreeNode(int x) : val(x), left(nullptr), right(nullptr) {}
};

// solution class
class Solution {
public:
    // find inorder successor in BST by tracking candidate
    TreeNode* inorderSuccessor(TreeNode* root, TreeNode* p) {
        // initialize successor pointer
        TreeNode* successor = nullptr;
        // traverse until null
        while (root != nullptr) {
            // move left while updating successor when root > p
            if (root->val > p->val) {
                successor = root;
                root = root->left;
            }
            // otherwise move right
            else {
                root = root->right;
            }
        }
        // return final successor (or null)
        return successor;
    }
};

// print inorder traversal
void printInOrder(TreeNode* root) {
    // base case for recursion
    if (root == nullptr) return;
    // traverse left subtree
    printInOrder(root->left);
    // print current node
    cout << root->val << " ";
    // traverse right subtree
    printInOrder(root->right);
}

// program entry
int main() {
    // construct BST
    TreeNode* root = new TreeNode(5);
    root->left = new TreeNode(3);
    root->right = new TreeNode(6);
    root->left->left = new TreeNode(2);
    root->left->right = new TreeNode(4);
    root->right->right = new TreeNode(7);

    // print BST inorder
    cout << "BST: ";
    printInOrder(root);
    cout << endl;

    // choose node p (value 4)
    TreeNode* p = root->left->right;

    // find successor
    Solution solution;
    TreeNode* successor = solution.inorderSuccessor(root, p);

    // print result
    if (successor != nullptr) {
        cout << "Inorder Successor of " << p->val << " is: " << successor->val << endl;
    } else {
        cout << "Inorder Successor of " << p->val << " does not exist." << endl;
    }

    // finish
    return 0;
}

    import java.util.*;

// define tree node structure
class TreeNode {
    // value of the node
    int val;
    // left child reference
    TreeNode left;
    // right child reference
    TreeNode right;
    // constructor
    TreeNode(int x) { val = x; }
}

// solution class
class Solution {
    // find inorder successor in BST by tracking candidate
    public TreeNode inorderSuccessor(TreeNode root, TreeNode p) {
        // initialize successor reference
        TreeNode successor = null;
        // traverse until null
        while (root != null) {
            // move left while updating successor when root > p
            if (root.val > p.val) {
                successor = root;
                root = root.left;
            }
            // otherwise move right
            else {
                root = root.right;
            }
        }
        // return final successor (or null)
        return successor;
    }
}

// separate main class
public class Main {
    // print inorder traversal
    static void printInOrder(TreeNode root) {
        // base case for recursion
        if (root == null) return;
        // traverse left subtree
        printInOrder(root.left);
        // print current node
        System.out.print(root.val + " ");
        // traverse right subtree
        printInOrder(root.right);
    }

    // program entry
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        // construct BST
        TreeNode root = new TreeNode(5);
        root.left = new TreeNode(3);
        root.right = new TreeNode(6);
        root.left.left = new TreeNode(2);
        root.left.right = new TreeNode(4);
        root.right.right = new TreeNode(7);

        // print BST inorder
        System.out.print("BST: ");
        printInOrder(root);
        System.out.println();

        // choose node p (value 4)
        TreeNode p = root.left.right;

        // find successor
        Solution solution = new Solution();
        TreeNode successor = solution.inorderSuccessor(root, p);

        // print result
        if (successor != null) {
            System.out.println("Inorder Successor of " + p.val + " is: " + successor.val);
        } else {
            System.out.println("Inorder Successor of " + p.val + " does not exist.");
        }
    }
}

    # define tree node structure
class TreeNode:
    # constructor
    def __init__(self, x: int):
        # value of the node
        self.val = x
        # left child reference
        self.left = None
        # right child reference
        self.right = None

# solution class
class Solution:
    # find inorder successor in BST by tracking candidate
    def inorderSuccessor(self, root: TreeNode, p: TreeNode) -> TreeNode | None:
        # initialize successor reference
        successor = None
        # traverse until null
        while root is not None:
            # move left while updating successor when root > p
            if root.val > p.val:
                successor = root
                root = root.left
            # otherwise move right
            else:
                root = root.right
        # return final successor (or None)
        return successor

# print inorder traversal
def print_in_order(root: TreeNode | None) -> None:
    # base case for recursion
    if root is None:
        return
    # traverse left subtree
    print_in_order(root.left)
    # print current node
    print(root.val, end=" ")
    # traverse right subtree
    print_in_order(root.right)

# program entry
def main():

    root.left = TreeNode(3)
    root.right = TreeNode(6)
    root.left.left = TreeNode(2)
    root.left.right = TreeNode(4)
    root.right.right = TreeNode(7)

    print_in_order(root)

    # find successor

    # print result

    else:
        print(f"Inorder Successor of {p.val} does not exist.")

# run main

    // define tree node structure
class TreeNode {
    // constructor
    constructor(x) {
        // value of the node
        this.val = x;
        // left child reference
        this.left = null;
        // right child reference
        this.right = null;
    }
}

// solution class
class Solution {
    // find inorder successor in BST by tracking candidate
    inorderSuccessor(root, p) {
        // initialize successor reference
        let successor = null;
        // traverse until null
        while (root !== null) {
        // move left while updating successor when root > p
        if (root.val > p.val) {
            successor = root;
            root = root.left;
        }
        // otherwise move right
        else {
            root = root.right;
        }
        }
        // return final successor (or null)
        return successor;
    }
}

// print inorder traversal
function printInOrder(root) {
    // base case for recursion
    if (root === null) return;
    // traverse left subtree
    printInOrder(root.left);
    // print current node
    process.stdout.write(root.val + " ");
    // traverse right subtree
    printInOrder(root.right);
}

// program entry
function main() {
    // construct BST
    const root = new TreeNode(5);
    root.left = new TreeNode(3);
    root.right = new TreeNode(6);
    root.left.left = new TreeNode(2);
    root.left.right = new TreeNode(4);
    root.right.right = new TreeNode(7);

    // print BST inorder
    process.stdout.write("BST: ");
    printInOrder(root);
    process.stdout.write("\n");

    // choose node p (value 4)
    const p = root.left.right;

    // find successor
    const solution = new Solution();
    const successor = solution.inorderSuccessor(root, p);

    // print result
    if (successor !== null) {
        console.log("Inorder Successor of " + p.val + " is: " + successor.val);
    } else {
        console.log("Inorder Successor of " + p.val + " does not exist.");
    }
}

// run main
main();
    Complexity Analysis

    Time Complexity: O(N) where N is the number of nodes in the binary search tree. This complexity arises from the fact that we have to traverse all nodes in an inorder fashion to get to the inorder successor.

    Space Complexity: O(1) as no additional data structure or memory allocation is done during the traversal and algorithm. Only a value comparison at each node.

    Optimal Approach
    Algorithm
    • Initialize a variable 'successor' as the maximum integer value.
    • Traverse the Binary Search Tree (BST) based on the key value.
    • If the current node value is smaller than the key, move to its right child.
    • If the current node value is greater than the key, update 'successor' to the minimum between the current 'successor' and the current node value, then move to the left child.
    • Continue this process until the traversal reaches a null node.

    Code

    #include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

// define tree node structure
struct TreeNode {
    // value of the node
    int val;
    // pointer to left child
    TreeNode* left;
    // pointer to right child
    TreeNode* right;
    // constructor
    TreeNode(int x) : val(x), left(nullptr), right(nullptr) {}
};

// solution class
class Solution {
public:
    // find inorder successor iteratively
    TreeNode* inorderSuccessor(TreeNode* root, TreeNode* p) {
        // initialize successor
        TreeNode* successor = nullptr;
        // traverse until root becomes null
        while (root != nullptr) {
            // when p is greater or equal, move right
            if (p->val >= root->val) {
                root = root->right;
            }
            // otherwise update successor and move left
            else {
                successor = root;
                root = root->left;
            }
        }
        // return successor (may be null)
        return successor;
    }
};

// inorder print helper
void printInOrder(TreeNode* root) {
    // base case
    if (root == nullptr) return;
    // traverse left
    printInOrder(root->left);
    // print node
    cout << root->val << " ";
    // traverse right
    printInOrder(root->right);
}

// program entry
int main() {
    // construct BST
    TreeNode* root = new TreeNode(5);
    root->left = new TreeNode(3);
    root->right = new TreeNode(6);
    root->left->left = new TreeNode(2);
    root->left->right = new TreeNode(4);
    root->right->right = new TreeNode(7);

    // show inorder
    cout << "BST: ";
    printInOrder(root);
    cout << endl;

    // target node p
    TreeNode* p = root->left->right;

    // find successor
    Solution solution;
    TreeNode* successor = solution.inorderSuccessor(root, p);

    // print result
    if (successor != nullptr) {
        cout << "Inorder Successor of " << p->val << " is: " << successor->val << endl;
    } else {
        cout << "Inorder Successor of " << p->val << " does not exist." << endl;
    }

    // exit
    return 0;
}

    import java.util.*;

// define tree node structure
class TreeNode {
    // value of the node
    int val;
    // left child reference
    TreeNode left;
    // right child reference
    TreeNode right;
    // constructor
    TreeNode(int x) { val = x; }
}

// solution class
class Solution {
    // find inorder successor iteratively
    public TreeNode inorderSuccessor(TreeNode root, TreeNode p) {
        // initialize successor
        TreeNode successor = null;
        // traverse until root becomes null
        while (root != null) {
            // when p is greater or equal, move right
            if (p.val >= root.val) {
                root = root.right;
            }
            // otherwise update successor and move left
            else {
                successor = root;
                root = root.left;
            }
        }
        // return successor (may be null)
        return successor;
    }
}

// separate main class
public class Main {
    // inorder print helper
    static void printInOrder(TreeNode root) {
        // base case
        if (root == null) return;
        // traverse left
        printInOrder(root.left);
        // print node
        System.out.print(root.val + " ");
        // traverse right
        printInOrder(root.right);
    }

    // program entry
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        // construct BST
        TreeNode root = new TreeNode(5);
        root.left = new TreeNode(3);
        root.right = new TreeNode(6);
        root.left.left = new TreeNode(2);
        root.left.right = new TreeNode(4);
        root.right.right = new TreeNode(7);

        // show inorder
        System.out.print("BST: ");
        printInOrder(root);
        System.out.println();

        // target node p
        TreeNode p = root.left.right;

        // find successor
        Solution solution = new Solution();
        TreeNode successor = solution.inorderSuccessor(root, p);

        // print result
        if (successor != null) {
            System.out.println("Inorder Successor of " + p.val + " is: " + successor.val);
        } else {
            System.out.println("Inorder Successor of " + p.val + " does not exist.");
        }
    }
}

    # define tree node structure
class TreeNode:
    # constructor
    def __init__(self, x: int):
        # value of the node
        self.val = x
        # left child reference
        self.left = None
        # right child reference
        self.right = None

# solution class
class Solution:
    # find inorder successor iteratively
    def inorderSuccessor(self, root: 'TreeNode', p: 'TreeNode') -> 'TreeNode | None':
        # initialize successor
        successor = None
        # traverse until root becomes None
        while root is not None:
            # when p is greater or equal, move right
            if p.val >= root.val:
                root = root.right
            # otherwise update successor and move left
            else:
                successor = root
                root = root.left
        # return successor (may be None)
        return successor

# inorder print helper
def print_in_order(root: 'TreeNode | None') -> None:
    # base case
    if root is None:
        return
    # traverse left
    print_in_order(root.left)
    # print node
    print(root.val, end=" ")
    # traverse right
    print_in_order(root.right)

# program entry

    # construct BST
    root = TreeNode(5)
    root.left = TreeNode(3)
    root.right = TreeNode(6)
    root.left.left = TreeNode(2)
    root.left.right = TreeNode(4)
    root.right.right = TreeNode(7)

    # show inorder
    print("BST: ", end="")
    print_in_order(root)
    print()

    # target node p
    p = root.left.right

    # find successor
    solution = Solution()
    successor = solution.inorderSuccessor(root, p)

    # print result
    if successor is not None:
        print(f"Inorder Successor of {p.val} is: {successor.val}")
    else:
        print(f"Inorder Successor of {p.val} does not exist.")

# run main

    // define tree node structure
class TreeNode {
    // constructor
    constructor(x) {
        // value of the node
        this.val = x;
        // left child reference
        this.left = null;
        // right child reference
        this.right = null;
    }
}

// solution class
class Solution {
    // find inorder successor iteratively
    inorderSuccessor(root, p) {
        // initialize successor
        let successor = null;
        // traverse until root becomes null
        while (root !== null) {
        // when p is greater or equal, move right
        if (p.val >= root.val) {
            root = root.right;
        }
        // otherwise update successor and move left
        else {
            successor = root;
            root = root.left;
        }
        }
        // return successor (may be null)
        return successor;
    }
}

// inorder print helper
function printInOrder(root) {
    // base case
    if (root === null) return;
    // traverse left
    printInOrder(root.left);
    // print node
    process.stdout.write(root.val + " ");
    // traverse right
    printInOrder(root.right);
}

// program entry
function main() {
    // construct BST
    const root = new TreeNode(5);
    root.left = new TreeNode(3);
    root.right = new TreeNode(6);
    root.left.left = new TreeNode(2);
    root.left.right = new TreeNode(4);
    root.right.right = new TreeNode(7);

    // show inorder
    process.stdout.write("BST: ");
    printInOrder(root);
    process.stdout.write("\n");

    // target node p
    const p = root.left.right;

    // find successor
    const solution = new Solution();
    const successor = solution.inorderSuccessor(root, p);

    // print result
    if (successor !== null) {
        console.log("Inorder Successor of " + p.val + " is: " + successor.val);
    } else {
        console.log("Inorder Successor of " + p.val + " does not exist.");
    }
}

// run main
main();
    Complexity Analysis

    Time Complexity: O(H) where H is the height of the binary search tree as we are traversing along the height of the tree.

    Space Complexity: O(1) as no additional data structure or memory allocation is done during the traversal and algorithm.
